question.title: Will the US BIS publish a final rule tightening semiconductor-related export controls before May 1, 2026?
question.description:
BIS export-control tightening can materially affect chip supply chains, revenues, and cross-border technology flows.

`{"format":"bot_tournament_question","info":{"hash_id":"f3caa84066df2777","sheet_id":302.0}}`
question.resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the Federal Register publishes a BIS final rule before May 1, 2026 whose summary indicates it adds new license requirements, expands the scope of controls, removes or narrows license exceptions, or otherwise increases restrictions specifically for advanced semiconductors and/or semiconductor manufacturing items. This question resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is the Federal Register BIS agency page at https://www.federalregister.gov/agencies/bureau-of-industry-and-security
question.fine_print: The rule must be a final rule (not a proposed rule, interim final rule without finalization, or a guidance-only document). If there is a reasonable dispute about whether a rule “tightens” controls under this definition, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions while prioritizing the Federal Register summary and rule text. Candidate Sources: BIS rulemaking page https://www.bis.gov/regulations
